steps.title: Pin down what the underlined portion does
steps.content: The sentence before the underlined portion states that Indigenous-led centers “contrast with museums that aren’t Indigenous-led.” That sets up a comparison but leaves it unspecified. The underlined portion fills in how those non-Indigenous-led museums differ: they target mainly non-Indigenous audiences and rely on Euro-centric exhibit design. In other words, it expands on — elaborates — the claim that was just made.

steps.title: Match the function to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It defines a term used earlier in the sentence.’: No term is being defined; no vocabulary word is explained here. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It addresses a potential criticism of a claim made earlier in the sentence.’: There is no criticism or counterargument being answered. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It elaborates on a claim made earlier in the sentence.’: The preceding clause claims a contrast exists; the underlined portion specifies the nature of that contrast, directly elaborating the claim. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It acknowledges an exception to the general principle proposed earlier in the sentence.’: The underlined portion reinforces the contrast, not carves out an exception to it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
